coach.hint: Chunk it: I told her that I could see that she cared about protecting her son, / and I asked her if

coach.hint: Chunk it: she got that from her parents.

coach.hint: Chunk it: I learned why she was afraid and angry, / and how she fought through that fear to make

items.line_en: a family.

coach.hint: Chunk it: a family.

items.line_en: It's not.

coach.say: It's not.
coach.hint: Chunk it: It's not.

items.line_en: It's right there.

coach.say: It's right there.
coach.hint: Chunk it: It's right there.

coach.hint: Chunk it: When we stop trying to figure out who deserves it, / and we welcome someone, / anyone, / exactly

items.line_en: as they are in the moment.

coach.say: as they are in the moment.
coach.hint: Chunk it: as they are in the moment.

items.line_en: It would be two more years before I learned to do this with all the parts of myself.

coach.say: It would be two more years before I learned to do this with all the parts of myself.
coach.hint: Chunk it: It would be two more years before I learned to do this with all the parts of myself.

items.line_en: But it started just as simply.

coach.say: But it started just as simply.
coach.hint: Chunk it: But it started just as simply.

items.line_en: A part of me had become very chatty.

coach.say: A part of me had become very chatty.
coach.hint: Chunk it: A part of me had become very chatty.

items.line_en: A part that was afraid and angry, whining, demanding, unreasonable, and relentless.

coach.hint: Chunk it: A part that was afraid and angry, whining, / demanding, unreasonable, and relentless.

items.line_en: She told me that we were never going to get better, and she wanted out of here.

coach.hint: Chunk it: She told me that we were never going to get better, / and she wanted out of here.

items.line_en: I asked her what I could do.

coach.say: I asked her what I could do.
coach.hint: Chunk it: I asked her what I could do.

items.line_en: She only told me she wanted to die over and over and over and over.

coach.hint: Chunk it: She only told me she wanted to die over and over and over and / over.

items.line_en: I begged her to shut up, and she did not.

coach.hint: Chunk it: I begged her to shut up, / and she did not.

coach.hint: Chunk it: Finally, / after weeks of harassment, / whether out of exhaustion or epiphany, / instead of

items.line_en: I became my own moderator.

coach.say: I became my own moderator.
coach.hint: Chunk it: I became my own moderator.

coach.hint: Chunk it: I said out loud, / and a voice that surprised me, / thank you for sharing.

coach.hint: Chunk it: I'm going to remind you of our agreement to be honest and / reasonable.

coach.hint: Chunk it: And she answered me, / that voice, / now don't get spooked.
